title: Shouldn't add insecure registries to list of search registries
body:
The search registries and insecure registries are separate lists,
and should not be merged just because the InsecureSkipTLSVerify is
set

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: <s>ACK as far as this one instance goes. There are quite a few other instances of the “ `alltransports.ParseImageName` or guess” pattern which might benefit similarly, but that does not really block improving this one.</s>

> ACK as far as

Please ignore, wrong tab.

:pushpin: Commit 8c9b4cc has been approved by `mtrmac`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mtrmac 8c9b4cca2fa0b4e00de089e83d06da44acad5e45 -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/c306342ee1390163c4f114c415a6ba27e84e3b04.1528380839276212480/index.html)
Approved by: mtrmac
Pushing c306342ee1390163c4f114c415a6ba27e84e3b04 to master...

That's what I was poking at.  I might have missed it, but I only see us reading the secured list from that file.  My thinking, maybe wrong, is we should read the secured list if tls-verify = true, the insecured list if tls-verify=false.

There _is no_ “secured list”; all registries are secure by default, unless they are in an insecure list.

The search list is, AFAIK, completely orthogonal to the secure/insecure statuses of registries

@mtrmac you're right, that is listed as "search" list in the registries.conf.  In my noggin I'd it as the secured list.  Regardless, I don't think we're ever reading the registries.insecure list in Buildah.  Do you have thoughts on when we should be including that list in our search of registires if ever?

Yeah, buildah might need to do something like https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/0eda60957d593411a144371bb4903c7a74307a59/libpod/image/pull.go#L194 .

Though, ideally (see the conversation around `sysregistriesv2`), containers/image should probably read the configuration automatically and use it for everyone, so that the code in libpod does not have to be copied all over the place.
